scenario: Sextant Bank runs a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot service with 12 workers spread across 5 clusters. The platform team must externalize the camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active property as an environment variable across every deployment so that operators can override the value without rebuilding the application, following Spring Boot relaxed binding conventions for environment variables.
question: Which environment variable name corresponds to the Camunda 8.8 property camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active under Spring Boot relaxed binding?

options.text: CAMUNDA_CLIENT_WORKER_DEFAULTS_MAXJOBSACTIVE is the environment variable name for the property camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active under Spring Boot relaxed binding conventions
CAMUNDA.CLIENT.WORKER.DEFAULTS.MAX-JOBS-ACTIVE is the environment variable name that maps to the property when Spring Boot preserves dots and dashes verbatim in the variable name itself
CAMUNDA_CLIENT_WORKER_DEFAULTS_MAX_JOBS_ACTIVE is the environment variable name that maps to the property when each dash in the property is converted into an underscore character
camunda_client_worker_defaults_maxJobsActive is the environment variable name that maps to the property when Spring Boot keeps original case and only replaces dots with underscores

a.text: Correct. Spring Boot relaxed binding converts the configuration property to uppercase, removes the dashes, and replaces every dot delimiter with an underscore, yielding CAMUNDA_CLIENT_WORKER_DEFAULTS_MAXJOBSACTIVE.

b.text: Incorrect. Environment variable names cannot contain dots or dashes; the documented Spring Boot conversion removes the dashes and replaces dot delimiters with underscores in the resulting name.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented conversion removes the dashes from the property segment rather than converting each one to an underscore; max-jobs-active becomes the joined token MAXJOBSACTIVE.

d.text: Incorrect. The documented conversion uppercases the whole name and removes the dashes; Spring Boot does not preserve the original camelCase or lowercase form for environment variables.

explanation: Configuration properties are mapped to environment variables by uppercasing the name, removing any dashes, and replacing dot delimiters with underscores; camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active therefore becomes CAMUNDA_CLIENT_WORKER_DEFAULTS_MAXJOBSACTIVE in the environment.
